items.correct: No - since 2006 it is a dwarf planet, because it has not cleared its orbit.
items.misconception: Yes, it is the ninth planet.
items.why: Pluto meets two of three planet criteria but shares its zone with other Kuiper Belt bodies.

items.q: What is the difference between an asteroid and a comet?
items.correct: Asteroids are rocky or metallic; comets are icy and grow tails near the Sun.
items.misconception: They are the same kind of object.
items.random_wrong: Comets are rocky and asteroids are icy.
items.why: Composition and location differ: rocky asteroids stay near the belt, icy comets vent gas near the Sun.

items.q: Why is Jupiter called a gas giant?
items.correct: It is huge and made mostly of hydrogen and helium with no solid surface.
items.misconception: Because it is the hottest planet.
items.random_wrong: Because it is closest to the Sun.
items.why: Its composition, not temperature or distance, makes it a gas giant.

items.q: Which of these is a terrestrial planet?
items.why: It's Mars. “Jupiter” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.correct: Ice sublimates near the Sun, releasing gas and dust
items.why: It's Ice sublimates near the Sun, releasing gas and dust. “It is on fire” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: The asteroid belt lies mainly between:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Mars and Jupiter. “Earth and Mars” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Pluto is classified as a dwarf planet because it:
items.correct: Has not cleared its orbital neighbourhood
items.why: It's Has not cleared its orbital neighbourhood. “Is too cold” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many planets are in the Solar System under the 2006 IAU definition?
items.why: Exact answer: 8 planets. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
